title: Trump Delays Threat to Iran, but War Negotiations Are in Early Stage
summary: President Trump postponed his threat to strike power plants in Iran, citing “productive conversations” with the Iranians. But officials said the talks were in an early stage and not substantive.

title: Trump Sours on British Leader Over Iran War: ‘What If Donald Shouts at Me?’
summary: President Trump once called Prime Minister Keir Starmer a friend. But Mr. Starmer’s decision not to join the attacks on Iran has led to merciless mocking by the president.

title: Pentagon Officials Weigh Deployment of Airborne Troops for Iran War
summary: The combat forces would come from a brigade of about 3,000 soldiers capable of deploying anywhere in the world within 18 hours.

title: In LaGuardia Crash That Killed 2, Call to ‘Stop!’ Came Too Late
summary: A collision between an Air Canada Express jet and a fire truck on Sunday night left two pilots dead and dozens injured.

title: FAA Is Investigating if Another Jet’s Issue Distracted LaGuardia Air Traffic Controller
summary: Two controllers were said to be working at LaGuardia Airport’s control center at the time of the crash that killed two pilots, and one had been dealing with an odor on a United Airlines plane.

title: Markwayne Mullin Confirmed as Homeland Security Secretary
summary: A Cherokee Nation member who has served as Oklahoma’s junior senator, Mr. Mullin will take charge at a pivotal time.

title: Mullin’s Smooth Confirmation Was a Throwback in the Senate
summary: In choosing Senator Markwayne Mullin, who has warm relationships across the Capitol and the political aisle, President Trump was reverting to a bygone tradition.

title: Trump, Who Calls Mail-in Voting ‘Cheating,’ Just Voted by Mail
summary: President Trump has long fixated on mail-in-voting to bolster his baseless claims of widespread voter fraud. But he recently used the method in a Florida special election.

title: Supreme Court Appears Poised to Reject Late-Arriving Mail-In Ballots Law
summary: A majority of the justices appeared skeptical of Mississippi’s mail-in ballot law in a case that could upend the way that states handle mail-in ballots throughout the country.

title: In California, a Republican Sheriff Seizes Ballots, Prompting Concerns
summary: Riverside County Sheriff Chad Bianco, a candidate for governor, recently took possession of more than 650,000 ballots as part of a fraud probe. Election officials say his investigation is baseless.

title: MDMA Therapy in Australia Shows Results for PTSD Patients, but the Cost Is Limiting Access
summary: The country’s experiment with psychedelic medicine has led to positive outcomes, psychiatrists say, but also highlights the limitations of the nascent field.

title: Pentagon Adopts New Limits for Journalists After Court Loss
summary: The Defense Department said it would close the Pentagon’s work area for journalists, among other changes, after a judge found the existing media policy unconstitutional.

title: Trump Administration to Pay $1 Billion to Energy Giant to Cancel Wind Farms
summary: In exchange, the French company TotalEnergies would invest in oil and natural gas projects in Texas and elsewhere.

title: Trump Administration Targets Harvard With Two New Investigations
summary: The inquiries target antisemitism and admissions policies. The university called it retaliation for refusing to give in to the administration’s demands.

title: ‘Who Doesn’t Love Elvis?’ Trump Takes an Unexpected Detour to Graceland.
summary: President Trump mused about whether he could beat Elvis Presley in a fistfight and used a golden Sharpie to sign a replica of one of the singer’s guitars.

title: Ukraine Spent Big to Shield Energy Industry From Drones. Is the Mideast Next?
summary: With the use of electronic jamming systems and interceptor drones, the Ukrainian national oil and gas company may be a model for others.

title: Pakistan Dials Up Its Information War
summary: New, friendly media operations and expanded state-run television are pushing Pakistan’s message while independent news outlets face repression.

title: Colombian Military Plane Crash Kills 66 and Injures Dozens
summary: A military aircraft transporting 128 people was involved in an accident as it took off from southern Colombia, according to the authorities.

title: Takeaways From The Times’s Inside Look at the C.D.C.
summary: Many current and former employees say the actions of Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. are undermining the agency’s role in safeguarding public health.

title: A Murder Charge in Georgia Exposes Complexities of the Abortion Debate
summary: A woman who took medication to induce an abortion, and then delivered the baby, was arrested on a murder charge. But on Monday, a state judge expressed deep skepticism about the case.

title: Valerie Perrine, Screen Siren Who Won Critical Acclaim, Dies at 82
summary: Known early on for skin-baring temptress roles, she later earned rave reviews, a Cannes award and an Oscar nomination for her performance in the Lenny Bruce biopic “Lenny.”

title: The United States Is No Longer the Leader of the Free World
summary: Pax Americana, meet Lax Americana.

title: ‘Everything After This Will Be Harder’: Gen. Stanley McChrystal on Iran
summary: David French talks with the retired general about the “great seduction” America fell for in Iran.

title: Late Night Doesn’t Feel So Hot About ICE at the Airport
summary: Jimmy Kimmel said President Trump had “found a way to make the airport even worse.”

title: Bill Cosby Loses Sex Assault Lawsuit and Faces a $59 Million Judgment
summary: A California jury found that Mr. Cosby had abused Donna Motsinger in 1972 after inviting her to attend one of his comedy shows.

title: Hasan Piker Defends Upscale Lodging During Humanitarian Mission in Cuba
summary: He helped bring attention to a group delivering humanitarian aid to Cuba, which was criticized for staying in an upscale hotel as Cubans faced another blackout.

title: Lilias Folan, Who Brought Yoga to Middle America, Dies at 90
summary: With her bright leotards and soothing, welcoming tone, she helped to demystify a discipline that many Americans in the 1970s viewed as a counterculture practice.

title: Boston University Pulls Pride Flags, Raising Free Speech Worries
summary: The university said the flags broke a rule against hanging signs, a policy embraced by other campuses that cracked down on protests. Professors and others say such rules chill speech.
